# 🔬 AUTONÓM MÉLYFÚRÁS (Gerilla RAG Deep Drill Report)
Adatbázis: Gerilla_RAG.db
Ezek azok a fejlett AI Agent, Adatbázis elemző és MCP eszközök, amiket a videomunka automatizálására/támogatására használhatunk.

## 📦 REPO: BOAZ_beta-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Stardust Modification: I added some functions to make it a PIC loader that can take arbitrary shellcode and compile to a PIC ready to be used. An modern 64-bit position independent implant template. - raw strings - global instance - compile time hashing

It leverages DXGI and Direct3D system libraries to enable extremely fast and robust screen capture functionality for your Python scripts and applications on Windows. **D3DShot:** * Is by far the fastest way to capture the screen with Python on Windows 8.1+ * Is very easy to use. ## 📦 REPO: PantheonRL-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** # PantheonRL PantheonRL is a package for training and testing multi-agent reinforcement learning environments. The goal of PantheonRL is to provide a modular and extensible framework for training agent policies, fine-tuning agent policies, ad-hoc pairing of agents, and more. PantheonRL also provides a web user interface suitable for lightweight experimentation and prototyping. PantheonRL is built on top of StableBaselines3 (SB3), allowing direct access to many of SB3's standard RL training algor...

## 📦 REPO: Probabilistic-Call-Stack-PoC-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # 🧬 Probabilistic Call Stack PoC > A proof-of-concept to demonstrate randomized execution paths and their impact on call stack signatures — ideal for EDR testing, behavior-based detection research, and evasion analysis. ## 📌 About This project showcases how a payload can be executed through **multiple unique call paths**, each producing a **distinct call stack**, even though the final behavior remains identical. The goal is to challenge **signature-based and stack-trace-based detection engines**...

## 📦 REPO: Process-Herpaderping-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # :syringe: Process-Herpaderping :syringe: My own implementation of the process herpaderping evasion technique discovered by [Johnny Shaw](https://github.com/jxy-s/herpaderping). ## 📦 REPO: al-khaser-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Struct Dump Codegen This is a LINQpad script to turn a struct definition into C++ code that prints out its contents. So far it supports USHORT, ULONG, and UCHAR. This was written specifically for IDENTIFY_DEVICE_DATA in order to speed up writing ATAIdentifyDump. This can probably be used for similar structs too, so it might be useful elsewhere.

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - *(Nincs specifikus dokumentált Python script, vizsgáld kézzel az iterrogatorral)*

----------------------------------------

## 📦 REPO: amiunique-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Am I Unique ? # This repository contains all the source code from the [AmIUnique.org](https://amiunique.org/) website. This application was built using the [framework Play 2.3](https://playframework.com/) for the back-end and [Bootstrap](http://getbootstrap.com/) for the front-end. ### Requirements ### **JDK8** is needed to build the application. ### Creation of the database ### A database is needed to store the fingerprints.

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - *(Nincs specifikus dokumentált Python script, vizsgáld kézzel az iterrogatorral)*

----------------------------------------

----------------------------------------

## 📦 REPO: camoufox-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Client Certificate test-certificates ## Server ```bash openssl req \ -x509 \ -newkey rsa:4096 \ -keyout server/server_key.pem \ -out server/server_cert.pem \

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/additions/juggler/NetworkObserver.js`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/build-tester/scripts/bundle.py`: *Manages the esbuild bundle of the checks library. Builds checks-bundle.js from TypeScript source on first run.*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/build-tester/scripts/certificate.py`: *Certificate generation (scoring + HMAC signing) and all ASCII output/display.*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/build-tester/scripts/constants.py`: *Shared constants, ANSI color codes, and simple formatting helpers.*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/build-tester/scripts/generate-presets.py`: *Generates per-context + global fingerprint configs using the Camoufox Python API. Called by the camoufox-tester to get realistic fingerprint data.  Output: JSON object to stdout with macPerContext, li...*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/build-tester/scripts/grading.py`: *Grading, check counting, match verification, and cross-profile analysis.*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/build-tester/scripts/presets.py`: *Fingerprint preset generation, injection, and profile config conversion.*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/build-tester/scripts/run_tests.py`: *Camoufox Build Tester — Python CLI  Runs the same antibot-detection checks as the Next.js web app, but as a standalone CLI with ASCII art certificate output.  Usage:   python scripts/run_tests.py <bin...*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/build-tester/scripts/runner.py`: *Playwright test runner — launches Camoufox, runs per-context and global profiles, collects results, and returns the full result dict.*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/build-tester/scripts/server.py`: *Local HTTP server that serves the test page and checks bundle to the browser.*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/build-tester/scripts/wsl.py`: *WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux) detection and path conversion helpers. Used when running a Linux Camoufox binary from a Windows host.*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/example/async_example.py`: *Async version of the example — useful for scraping multiple pages concurrently.  Install deps:     pip install cloverlabs-camoufox     python -m camoufox fetch*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/example/example.py`: *Quick example to test cloverlabs-camoufox.  Install deps:     pip install cloverlabs-camoufox     python -m camoufox fetch*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/jsonvv/jsonvv/__main__.py`: *Load and parse a JSON file.*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/jsonvv/jsonvv/exceptions.py`: *Exception classes for jsonvv*

  - 📄 `camoufox-main/pythonlib/camoufox/__main__.py`: *CLI package manager for Camoufox*

  - 📄 `camoufox-main/pythonlib/camoufox/_warnings.py`: *Raised when a the user has a setting enabled that can cause detection.*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/pythonlib/camoufox/addons.py`: *Default addons to be downloaded*

  - 📄 `camoufox-main/pythonlib/camoufox/exceptions.py`: *Raised when the Camoufox executable is outdated.*

  - 📄 `camoufox-main/pythonlib/camoufox/geolocation.py`: *Helpers to fetch geolocation, timezone, and locale data given an IP*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/pythonlib/camoufox/gui/__init__.py`: *Camouman - QML-based GUI for managing Camoufox versions*

  - 📄 `camoufox-main/pythonlib/camoufox/ip.py`: *Helpers to find the user's public IP address for geolocation.*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/pythonlib/camoufox/locales.py`: *Data structures for locale and geolocation info*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/pythonlib/camoufox/multiversion.py`: *Manager for handling multiple Camoufox versions side by side*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/pythonlib/camoufox/pkgman.py`: *Print a styled message*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/pythonlib/camoufox/server.py`: *Convert a string to camelCase*
  - 📄 `camoufox-main/pythonlib/camoufox/sync_api.py`: *Wrapper around playwright.sync_api.PlaywrightContextManager that automatically     launches a browser and closes it when the context manager is exited.*

  - 📄 `causalml-master/causalml/feature_selection/filters.py`: *Filter feature selection methods for uplift modeling  - Currently only for classification problem: the outcome variable of uplift model is binary.*
  - 📄 `causalml-master/causalml/features.py`: *Label Encoder that groups infrequent values into one label.      Code from https://github.com/jeongyoonlee/Kaggler/blob/master/kaggler/preprocessing/data.py      Attributes:         min_obs (int): min...*
  - 📄 `causalml-master/causalml/inference/iv/drivlearner.py`: *A parent class for DRIV-learner regressor classes.      A DRIV-learner estimates endogenous treatment effects for compliers with machine learning models.      Details of DR-learner are available at `K...*
  - 📄 `causalml-master/causalml/inference/iv/iv_regression.py`: *A wrapper class that uses IV2SLS from statsmodel      A linear 2SLS model that estimates the average treatment effect with endogenous treatment variable.*
  - 📄 `causalml-master/causalml/inference/meta/base.py`: *Runs a single bootstrap. Fits on bootstrapped sample, then predicts on whole population.*
  - 📄 `causalml-master/causalml/inference/meta/drlearner.py`: *A parent class for DR-learner regressor classes.      A DR-learner estimates treatment effects with machine learning models.      Details of DR-learner are available at `Kennedy (2020) <https://arxiv....*
  - 📄 `causalml-master/causalml/inference/meta/explainer.py`: *The Explainer class handles all feature explanation/interpretation functions, including plotting         feature importances, shapley value distributions, and shapley value dependency plots.          ...*
  - 📄 `causalml-master/causalml/inference/meta/rlearner.py`: *A parent class for R-learner classes.      An R-learner estimates treatment effects with two machine learning models and the propensity score.      Details of R-learner are available at `Nie and Wager...*
  - 📄 `causalml-master/causalml/inference/meta/slearner.py`: *A sklearn style wrapper class for statsmodels' OLS.*
  - 📄 `causalml-master/causalml/inference/meta/tlearner.py`: *A parent class for T-learner regressor classes.      A T-learner estimates treatment effects with two machine learning models.      Details of T-learner are available at `Kunzel et al. (2018) <https:/...*
  - 📄 `causalml-master/causalml/inference/meta/tmle.py`: *Calculate the ATE and variances with the simplified TMLE method.      Args:         y (numpy.array): an outcome vector         w (numpy.array): a treatment vector         q0w (numpy.array): an outcome...*
  - 📄 `causalml-master/causalml/inference/meta/utils.py`: *Translate objective to be compatible with loaded xgboost version      Args     ----      objective : string         The objective to translate.      Returns     -------     The translated objective, o...*
  - 📄 `causalml-master/causalml/inference/meta/xlearner.py`: *A parent class for X-learner regressor classes.      An X-learner estimates treatment effects with four machine learning models.      Details of X-learner are available at `Kunzel et al. (2018) <https...*
  - 📄 `causalml-master/causalml/inference/tf/dragonnet.py`: *This module implements the Dragonnet [1], which adapts the design and training of neural networks to improve the quality of treatment effect estimates. The authors propose two adaptations:  - A new ar...*
  - 📄 `causalml-master/causalml/inference/tf/utils.py`: *Implements a classification (binary cross-entropy) loss function for DragonNet architecture.      Args:         - concat_true (tf.tensor): tensor of true samples, with shape (n_samples, 2)            ...*
  - 📄 `causalml-master/causalml/inference/torch/cevae.py`: *This module calls the CEVAE[1] function implemented by pyro team. CEVAE demonstrates a number of innovations including:  - A generative model for causal effect inference with hidden confounders; - A m...*

## 📦 REPO: chroma-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Embedding Function Schemas This directory contains JSON schemas for all embedding functions in Chroma. The purpose of having these schemas is to support cross-language compatibility and to validate that changes in one client library do not accidentally diverge from others. ## Schema Structure Each schema follows the JSON Schema Draft-07 specification and includes: - `version`: The version of the schema - `title`: The title of the schema

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**

## 📦 REPO: context7-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Context7 Plugin for Claude Code Context7 solves a common problem with AI coding assistants: outdated training data and hallucinated APIs. Instead of relying on stale knowledge, Context7 fetches current documentation directly from source repositories. ## What's Included This plugin provides: - **MCP Server** - Connects Claude Code to Context7's documentation service - **Skills** - Auto-triggers documentation lookups when you ask about libraries

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `context7-master/packages/tools-ai-sdk/src/agents/context7.ts`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `context7-master/packages/tools-ai-sdk/src/agents/index.ts`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `context7-master/plugins/claude/context7/agents/docs-researcher.md`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `context7-master/server.json`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)

Navigate to `http://localhost:4200/`. The app will automatically reload if you change any of the source files. ## Code scaffolding

## 📦 REPO: crawlee-python-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Unit tests Some tests may exhibit flaky behavior in CI. The reason for flaky behavior should be understood as it can indicate bug in the code or design flaw in the test. There are other reasons related to test execution, such as some tests that are not (or can not be) properly isolated, or limited resource constraints of the test executor. Here are some suggested approaches to mitigate flakiness, sorted in the order of preference: - Investigate the root cause and fix the code or test. - Apply ...

## 📦 REPO: dowhy-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Community notebooks This folder contains notebooks showing use-cases of DoWhy in different applications. These notebooks are contributed by users of DoWhy. ## How to contribute If you would like to contribute a notebook, raise a Pull Request that adds a new notebook to this folder. For help with raising a PR, refer to the [instructions for contributing code](https://github.com/py-why/dowhy/blob/main/docs/source/contributing/contributing-code.rst). Make sure that your notebook conveys the follo...

  - 📄 `dowhy-main/dowhy/api/causal_data_frame.py`: *An accessor for the pandas.DataFrame under the `causal` namespace.          :param pandas_obj:*
  - 📄 `dowhy-main/dowhy/causal_estimator.py`: *Base class for an estimator of causal effect.      Subclasses implement different estimation methods. All estimation methods are in the package "dowhy.causal_estimators"*
  - 📄 `dowhy-main/dowhy/causal_estimators/causalml.py`: *Wrapper class for estimators from the causalml library.      Supports additional parameters as listed below. For specific     parameters of each estimator, refer to the CausalML docs.*
  - 📄 `dowhy-main/dowhy/causal_estimators/distance_matching_estimator.py`: *Simple matching estimator for binary treatments based on a distance     metric.      Supports additional parameters as listed below.*
  - 📄 `dowhy-main/dowhy/causal_estimators/doubly_robust_estimator.py`: *Doubly Robust Estimator for Causal Effect Estimation.      Supports any RegressionEstimator for the regression stage, and accepts     a propensity score model and column for the propensity score stage...*
  - 📄 `dowhy-main/dowhy/causal_estimators/econml.py`: *Wrapper class for estimators from the EconML library.      Supports additional parameters as listed below. For init and fit     parameters of each estimator, refer to the EconML docs.*
  - 📄 `dowhy-main/dowhy/causal_estimators/generalized_linear_model_estimator.py`: *Compute effect of treatment using a generalized linear model such as logistic regression.      Implementation uses statsmodels.api.GLM.     Needs an additional parameter, "glm_family" to be specified ...*
  - 📄 `dowhy-main/dowhy/causal_estimators/instrumental_variable_estimator.py`: *Compute effect of treatment using the instrumental variables method.      This is also a superclass that can be inherited by other specific methods.      Supports additional parameters as listed below...*
  - 📄 `dowhy-main/dowhy/causal_estimators/linear_regression_estimator.py`: *Compute effect of treatment using linear regression.      Fits a regression model for estimating the outcome using treatment(s) and confounders. For a univariate treatment, the treatment effect is equ...*
  - 📄 `dowhy-main/dowhy/causal_estimators/propensity_score_estimator.py`: *Base class for estimators that estimate effects based on propensity of     treatment assignment.      Supports additional parameters as listed below.*
  - 📄 `dowhy-main/dowhy/causal_estimators/propensity_score_matching_estimator.py`: *Estimate effect of treatment by finding matching treated and control     units based on propensity score.      Straightforward application of the back-door criterion.      Supports additional paramete...*
  - 📄 `dowhy-main/dowhy/causal_estimators/propensity_score_stratification_estimator.py`: *Estimate effect of treatment by stratifying the data into bins with     identical common causes.      Straightforward application of the back-door criterion.      Supports additional parameters as lis...*
  - 📄 `dowhy-main/dowhy/causal_estimators/propensity_score_weighting_estimator.py`: *Estimate effect of treatment by weighing the data by     inverse probability of occurrence.      Straightforward application of the back-door criterion.      Supports additional parameters as listed b...*
  - 📄 `dowhy-main/dowhy/causal_estimators/regression_discontinuity_estimator.py`: *Compute effect of treatment using the regression discontinuity method.      Estimates effect by transforming the problem to an instrumental variables     problem.      Supports additional parameters a...*
  - 📄 `dowhy-main/dowhy/causal_estimators/regression_estimator.py`: *Compute effect of treatment using some regression function.      Fits a regression model for estimating the outcome using treatment(s) and     confounders.      Base class for all regression models, i...*
  - 📄 `dowhy-main/dowhy/causal_estimators/two_stage_regression_estimator.py`: *Compute treatment effect whenever the effect is fully mediated by     another variable (front-door) or when there is an instrument available.      Currently only supports a linear model for the effect...*

## 📦 REPO: ebpf-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # `cilium/ebpf` LLVM Builder Image This is a simple Docker image to provide reproducible eBPF ELF builds across contributors' workstations. This standardizes on a single environment used to regenerate e.g. testdata ELFs and does not depend on the toolchain installed on the host machine. Additionally, it reduces drift in the bytecode committed to the repository over time as the same exact clang + llc version is used throughout the development lifecycle. Only when upgrading or rebuilding the Docke...

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `ebpf-main/docs/macros.py`: *Macro definitions for documentation.*

  - 📄 `fabric-main/fabric/testing/fixtures.py`: *`pytest <https://pytest.org>`_ fixtures for easy use of Fabric test helpers.  To get Fabric plus this module's dependencies (as well as those of the main `fabric.testing.base` module which these fixtu...*

## 📦 REPO: freqtrade-develop
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Freqtrade Client # ![freqtrade](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freqtrade/freqtrade/develop/docs/assets/freqtrade_poweredby.svg) Provides a minimal rest client for the freqtrade rest api. Please check out the [main project](https://github.com/freqtrade/freqtrade) for more information or details.

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/build_helpers/create_command_partials.py`: *Capture the help output from a parser.*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/build_helpers/extract_config_json_schema.py`: *Script to extract the configuration json schema from config_schema.py file.*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/docs/bot-basics.md`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/docs/bot-usage.md`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/docs/commands/list-freqaimodels.md`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/docs/commands/webserver.md`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/__init__.py`: *Freqtrade bot*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/__main__.py`: *__main__.py for Freqtrade To launch Freqtrade as a module  > python -m freqtrade (with Python >= 3.11)*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/commands/__init__.py`: *Commands module. Contains all start-commands, subcommands and CLI Interface creation.  Note: Be careful with file-scoped imports in these subfiles.     as they are parsed on startup, nothing containin...*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/commands/analyze_commands.py`: *Start analysis script     :param args: Cli args from Arguments()     :return: None*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/commands/arguments.py`: *This module contains the argument manager class*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/commands/build_config_commands.py`: *Create a new strategy from a template     Asking the user questions to fill out the template accordingly.*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/commands/cli_options.py`: *Definition of cli arguments used in arguments.py*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/commands/data_commands.py`: *Download data (former download_backtest_data.py script)*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/commands/deploy_commands.py`: *Create "user_data" directory to contain user data strategies, hyperopt, ...)     :param args: Cli args from Arguments()     :return: None*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/commands/hyperopt_commands.py`: *List hyperopt epochs previously evaluated*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/commands/list_commands.py`: *Print available exchanges     :param args: Cli args from Arguments()     :return: None*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/commands/optimize_commands.py`: *Prepare the configuration for the Hyperopt module     :param args: Cli args from Arguments()     :param method: Bot running mode     :return: Configuration*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/commands/pairlist_commands.py`: *Test Pairlist configuration*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/commands/plot_commands.py`: *Entrypoint for dataframe plotting*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/commands/strategy_utils_commands.py`: *Start the strategy updating script     :param args: Cli args from Arguments()     :return: None*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/commands/trade_commands.py`: *Main entry point for trading mode*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/commands/webserver_commands.py`: *Main entry point for webserver mode*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/configuration/config_secrets.py`: *Remove sensitive information from the config.     :param config: Configuration     :param show_sensitive: Show sensitive information     :return: Configuration*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/configuration/config_setup.py`: *Prepare the configuration for utils subcommands     :param args: Cli args from Arguments()     :param method: Bot running mode     :return: Configuration*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/configuration/config_validation.py`: *Extended validator for the Freqtrade configuration JSON Schema.     Currently it only handles defaults for subschemas.*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/configuration/configuration.py`: *This module contains the configuration class*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/configuration/deploy_config.py`: *Ask user a few questions to build the configuration.     Interactive questions built using https://github.com/tmbo/questionary     :returns: Dict with keys to put into template*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/configuration/deprecated_settings.py`: *Functions to handle deprecated settings*

  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/configuration/load_config.py`: *This module contain functions to load the configuration file*

  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/util/formatters.py`: *Helper method getting decimal amount for this coin     example usage: f".{decimals_per_coin('USD')}f"     :param coin: Which coin are we printing the price / value for*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/util/ft_precise.py`: *Slim wrapper around ccxt's Precise (string math) To have imports from freqtrade - and support float initializers*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/util/ft_ttlcache.py`: *A TTLCache with a different default timer to allow for easier mocking in tests.*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/util/measure_time.py`: *Measure the time of a block of code and call a callback if the time limit is exceeded.*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/util/migrations/__init__.py`: *Migrate persisted data from old formats to new formats*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/util/periodic_cache.py`: *Special cache that expires at "straight" times     A timer with ttl of 3600 (1h) will expire at every full hour (:00).*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/util/progress_tracker.py`: *Get progress Bar with custom columns.*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/util/singleton.py`: *A thread-safe implementation of Singleton.     Use as metaclass to create singleton classes.*
  - 📄 `freqtrade-develop/freqtrade/util/template_renderer.py`: *Jinja2 rendering utils, used to generate new strategy and configurations.*

## 📦 REPO: generative-mouse-trajectories-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** ## mouse-telemetry **DISCLAIMER: This is intended for benevolent analytics and machine learning tasks; Do NOT use this for nefarious purposes** High-fidelity mouse telemetry collector for Rust. Captures cursor movement, clicks, and scrolls; computes derived kinematics (velocity, acceleration, jerk, smoothness, Fitts-style indices); and writes everything to CSV for analysis. ### Highlights - **Event capture**: Cursor moves, left/middle/right clicks, wheel up/down - **Derived metrics**: Velocity, ...

## 📦 REPO: hummingbot-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** ## Injective v2 This is a spot connector created by **[Injective Labs](https://injectivelabs.org/)**. The difference with `injective` connector is that v2 is a pure Python connector. That means that the user does not need to configure and run a Gateway instance to use the connector. The connector supports two different account modes: - Trading with delegate accounts - Trading through off-chain vault contracts There is a third account type called `read_only_account`. This mode only allows to requ...

## 📦 REPO: kungfu-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # mdmaker: Market Data Generator This is a set of python scripts that create random market data, useful for testing. ## Usage `stock.py` generates a csv with the top of book on each line with a valid timestamp. `merge.py` merges two top-of-book csvs using the timestamps for the operations.

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `kungfu-main/extensions/sim/src/python/sim/mdmaker/src/stock.py`: *Market data generator script.*
  - 📄 `kungfu-main/framework/core/conanfile.py`: *Updates mtime of lock files for node-gyp sake*
  - 📄 `kungfu-main/framework/core/src/python/kungfu/console/bridging/pdm/__init__.py`: *Make up pyproject.toml according to settings in package.json*
  - 📄 `kungfu-main/framework/core/src/python/kungfu/console/commands/__init__.py`: *reorder the list of commands when listing the help*
  - 📄 `kungfu-main/framework/core/src/python/kungfu/console/site.py`: *Set all module __file__ and __cached__ attributes to an absolute path*
  - 📄 `kungfu-main/framework/core/src/python/setup.py`: *Distribution which always forces a binary package with platform name*

----------------------------------------

## 📦 REPO: logfire-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Release Instructions This applies to maintainers preparing a new release. ## Semi-automated release process ### Prerequisites: * The `gh` cli is installed and ready for use: * See installation instructions [here](https://github.com/cli/cli#installation)

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `logfire-main/docs/how-to-guides/mcp-server.md`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `logfire-main/docs/integrations/llms/claude-agent-sdk.md`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `logfire-main/docs/integrations/llms/magentic.md`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `logfire-main/docs/plugins/main.py`: *Called on each file after it is read and before it is converted to HTML.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/examples/python/flask-sqlalchemy/app/__init__.py`: *Store calculation information.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire-api/logfire_api/__init__.py`: *Show versions of logfire, OS and related packages.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/__init__.py`: ***Logfire** is the observability tool focused on developer experience.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/__main__.py`: *Logfire entry point.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/__init__.py`: *Internal Logfire logic.  We use the `_internal` module to discourage imports from outside the package, and thereby avoid causing breaking changes when refactoring the package.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/ast_utils.py`: *Values passed to `logfire.instrument` and/or values stored in a logfire instance as basic configuration.      These determine the arguments passed to the method calls added by the AST transformer.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/async_.py`: *Log a warning whenever a function running in the asyncio event loop blocks for too long.      See Logfire.log_slow_async_callbacks.     Inspired by https://gitlab.com/quantlane/libs/aiodebug.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/auth.py`: *Folder used to store global configuration, and user tokens.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/auto_trace/__init__.py`: *Install automatic tracing.      See `Logfire.install_auto_tracing` for more information.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/auto_trace/import_hook.py`: *The import hook entry point, inserted into `sys.meta_path` to apply AST rewriting to matching modules.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/auto_trace/rewrite_ast.py`: *Compile a modified AST of the module's source code in the module's namespace.      Returns a function which accepts module globals and executes the compiled code.      The modified AST wraps the body ...*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/auto_trace/types.py`: *Information about a module being imported that should maybe be traced automatically.      This object will be passed to a function that should return True if the module should be traced.     In partic...*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/baggage.py`: *Get all OpenTelemetry baggage for the current context as a mapping of key/value pairs.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/cli/__init__.py`: *The CLI for Pydantic Logfire.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/cli/auth.py`: *Authenticate with Logfire.      This will authenticate your machine with Logfire and store the credentials.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/cli/prompt.py`: *Prompt command for Logfire CLI.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/cli/run.py`: *Check if uv package manager is installed and available in the PATH.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/client.py`: *A Logfire HTTP client to interact with the API.      Args:         user_token: The user token to use when authenticating against the API.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/collect_system_info.py`: *Retrieve the package information for all installed packages.      Returns:         A dicts with the package name and version.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/config.py`: *Default base URL for the Logfire API.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/config_params.py`: *Possible values for the `pydantic_plugin_record` parameter.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/constants.py`: *Namespace within OTEL attributes used by logfire.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/db_statement_summary.py`: *Try to construct a useful span message from OTel db statement.      Returns: A new string to use as span message or None to keep the original message.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/exporters/__init__.py`: *The span exporters for Logfire.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/exporters/console.py`: *Console exporter for OpenTelemetry.  Inspired by https://opentelemetry-python.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_modules/opentelemetry/sdk/trace/export.html#ConsoleSpanExporter*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/exporters/dynamic_batch.py`: *A wrapper around a BatchSpanProcessor that dynamically adjusts the schedule delay.      The initial schedule delay is set to 100ms, and after processing 10 spans, it is set to the value of     the `OT...*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/exporters/logs.py`: *Checks if instrumentation is suppressed, then suppresses instrumentation itself.      Placed at the root of the tree of processors.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/exporters/otlp.py`: *A requests.Session subclass that defers failed requests to a DiskRetryer.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/exporters/processor_wrapper.py`: *Checks if instrumentation is suppressed, then suppresses instrumentation itself.      Placed at the root of the tree of processors.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/exporters/quiet_metrics.py`: *A MetricExporter that catches request exceptions to prevent OTEL from logging a huge traceback.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/exporters/remove_pending.py`: *An exporter that filters out pending spans if the corresponding final span is already in the same batch.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/exporters/test.py`: *A SpanExporter that stores exported spans in a list for asserting in tests.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/exporters/wrapper.py`: *A base class for SpanExporters that wrap another exporter.*
  - 📄 `logfire-main/logfire/_internal/formatter.py`: *Copied from `string.Formatter._vformat` https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/v3.11.4/Lib/string.py#L198-L247 then altered.*

## 📦 REPO: mitmproxy-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Quick Start - Install mitmproxy as described in [`../CONTRIBUTING.md`](../CONTRIBUTING.md) - Run `node --version` to make sure that you have at least Node.js 24 or above. - Run `cd ./web` to change to the directory with package.json. - Run `npm install` to install dependencies. - Run `uv run mitmweb` - Run `npm start` in a separate window to start the Vite development server for the web interface. ## Testing

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `mitmproxy-main/docs/bucketassets/robots.txt`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)

  - 📄 `mlfinlab-master/mlfinlab/util/misc.py`: *Various useful functions*
  - 📄 `mlfinlab-master/mlfinlab/util/multiprocess.py`: *Contains the logic from chapter 20 on multiprocessing and vectorization.*
  - 📄 `mlfinlab-master/mlfinlab/util/volatility.py`: *Various volatility estimators*
  - 📄 `mlfinlab-master/mlfinlab/util/volume_classifier.py`: *Volume classification methods (BVC and tick rule)*

## 📦 REPO: open_spiel-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** # OpenSpiel Rust API *Note:* This API is no longer maintained. This is a basic [Rust](https://www.rust-lang.org/) API for OpenSpiel. Please note that it is currently experimental and may not work as expected. If you use it, please report any issues. Fixes and improvements are more than welcome! See the `CMakeLists.txt` to see how it is setup: a dynamic shared library is created similarly to python extension (`librust_spiel.so`). A simple rust crate

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**

## 📦 REPO: pafish-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** <img alt="pafish-logo" src="pafish/pafish.ico" align="left" width="64px"> # Pafish Pafish is a testing tool that uses different techniques to detect virtual machines and malware analysis environments in the same way that malware families do. The project is free and open source; the code of all the anti-analysis techniques is publicly available. Pafish executables for Windows (x86 32-bit and 64-bit) can be downloaded from the [releases page](https://github.com/a0rtega/pafish/releases). ![pafish s...

  - *(Nincs specifikus dokumentált Python script, vizsgáld kézzel az iterrogatorral)*

  - 📄 `paramiko-main/demos/forward.py`: *Sample script showing how to do local port forwarding over paramiko.  This script connects to the requested SSH server and sets up local port forwarding (the openssh -L option) from a local port throu...*
  - 📄 `paramiko-main/demos/rforward.py`: *Sample script showing how to do remote port forwarding over paramiko.  This script connects to the requested SSH server and sets up remote port forwarding (the openssh -R option) from a remote port th...*
  - 📄 `paramiko-main/paramiko/_winapi.py`: *Windows API functions implemented as ctypes functions and classes as found in jaraco.windows (3.4.1).  If you encounter issues with this module, please consider reporting the issues in jaraco.windows ...*
  - 📄 `paramiko-main/paramiko/agent.py`: *SSH Agent interface*
  - 📄 `paramiko-main/paramiko/auth_handler.py`: *`.AuthHandler`*
  - 📄 `paramiko-main/paramiko/auth_strategy.py`: *Modern, adaptable authentication machinery.  Replaces certain parts of `.SSHClient`. For a concrete implementation, see the ``OpenSSHAuthStrategy`` class in `Fabric <https://fabfile.org>`_.*
  - 📄 `paramiko-main/paramiko/ber.py`: *Robey's tiny little attempt at a BER decoder.*
  - 📄 `paramiko-main/paramiko/buffered_pipe.py`: *Attempt to generalize the "feeder" part of a `.Channel`: an object which can be read from and closed, but is reading from a buffer fed by another thread.  The read operations are blocking and can have...*
  - 📄 `paramiko-main/paramiko/channel.py`: *Abstraction for an SSH2 channel.*

  - 📄 `paramiko-main/paramiko/common.py`: *Common constants and global variables.*
  - 📄 `paramiko-main/paramiko/compress.py`: *Compression implementations for a Transport.*
  - 📄 `paramiko-main/paramiko/config.py`: *Configuration file (aka ``ssh_config``) support.*
  - 📄 `paramiko-main/paramiko/ecdsakey.py`: *ECDSA keys*

  - 📄 `paramiko-main/paramiko/file.py`: *Reusable base class to implement Python-style file buffering around a     simpler stream.*

  - 📄 `paramiko-main/paramiko/kex_group1.py`: *Standard SSH key exchange ("kex" if you wanna sound cool).  Diffie-Hellman of 1024 bit key halves, using a known "p" prime and "g" generator.*
  - 📄 `paramiko-main/paramiko/kex_group14.py`: *Standard SSH key exchange ("kex" if you wanna sound cool).  Diffie-Hellman of 2048 bit key halves, using a known "p" prime and "g" generator.*

  - 📄 `paramiko-main/tests/test_util.py`: *Some unit tests for utility functions.*

## 📦 REPO: pupa-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Pupa: A legislative data scraping framework ![example workflow](https://github.com/opencivicdata/pupa/actions/workflows/package.yml/badge.svg?branch=master)

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/cli/commands/clean.py`: *Finds and returns all subclasses of an abstract class.*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/cli/commands/update.py`: *Retrieve custom entity importer, if configured, else packaged default.*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/exceptions.py`: *Base class for exceptions from within Pupa*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/importers/base.py`: *recursively hash unhashable objects*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/importers/organizations.py`: *an override for prepare imports that sorts the imports         by parent_id dependencies*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/importers/people.py`: *Whenever we do a Pseudo ID lookup from the database, we need to limit         based on the memberships -> organization -> jurisdiction, so we scope         the resolution.*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/models.py`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/scrape/base.py`: *Base class for all scrapers*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/scrape/bill.py`: *An Open Civic Data bill.*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/scrape/event.py`: *Details for an event in .format*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/scrape/jurisdiction.py`: *Base class for a jurisdiction*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/scrape/popolo.py`: *A popolo-style Post*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/scrape/schemas/bill.py`: *Schema for bill objects.*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/scrape/schemas/event.py`: *Schema for event objects.*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/scrape/vote_event.py`: *A functor for applying order to voteEvents.     A single OrderVoteEvent instance should be used for all bills in a scrape.     The vote events of each bill must be processed in chronological order,   ...*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/tests/importers/test_membership_importer.py`: *this is a mock importer that implements a resolve_json_id that is     just a pass-through*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/tests/importers/test_topsort.py`: *Hash a cycle, useful for comparing sets of cycles.      This checks the sorted set of each of the nodes in the cycle. This     is *not* a perfect check, but it's useful so that we can create a set    ...*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/tests/importers/test_vote_event_importer.py`: *this is a mock importer that implements a resolve_json_id that is     just a pass-through*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/tests/scrape/test_bill_scrape.py`: *Test that we can create an invalid bill, and validation will fail*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/tests/scrape/test_model_basics.py`: *a generic model used for testing the base and mixins*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/tests/scrape/test_people_org_scrape.py`: *Test that we can hack posts in on the fly'*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/tests/scrape/test_scraper.py`: *tests that scrapers that yield iterables work OK*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/tests/update/test_importer_resolution.py`: *Create a module object at runtime with a single class inside, and insert it     into sys.modules so import_string() can load it by dotted path.*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/utils/generic.py`: *pseudo ids are just JSON*
  - 📄 `pupa-master/pupa/utils/topsort.py`: *This exception is raised if the graph is Cyclic (or rather, when the     sorting algorithm *knows* that the graph is Cyclic by hitting a snag     in the top-sort)*

----------------------------------------

## 📦 REPO: pyclick-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** # pyclick This is a library for generating human-like mouse movements. The movements are based on the concept of bezier curve: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/B%C3%A9zier_curve

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `pyclick-master/pyclick/_beziercurve.py`: *Returns the binomial coefficient "n choose k"*
  - 📄 `pyclick-master/pyclick/humancurve.py`: *Generates a human-like mouse curve starting at given source point,     and finishing in a given destination point*

----------------------------------------

## 📦 REPO: pyod-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** All datasets stored in this folder are downloaded from **Outlier Detection DataSets (ODDS)**: http://odds.cs.stonybrook.edu/#table1 If you use any data here, please see ODDS' citation policy: Shebuti Rayana (2016).  ODDS Library [http://odds.cs.stonybrook.edu]. Stony Brook, NY: Stony Brook University, Department of Computer Science.

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `pyod-master/docs/model_persistence.rst`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `pyod-master/docs/pyod.models.rst`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `pyod-master/examples/abod_example.py`: *Example of using Angle-base outlier detection (ABOD) for outlier detection*
  - 📄 `pyod-master/examples/ae1svm_example.py`: *Example of using AE1SVM for outlier detection (pytorch)*
  - 📄 `pyod-master/examples/alad_example.py`: *Example of using Adversarially Learned Anomaly Detection (ALAD) for outlier detection*
  - 📄 `pyod-master/examples/auto_encoder_example.py`: *Example of using AutoEncoder for outlier detection*
  - 📄 `pyod-master/examples/auto_model_selection_example/auto_selection.ipynb`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `pyod-master/examples/auto_model_selection_example/model_info_summarizer.ipynb`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `pyod-master/examples/auto_model_selection_example/readme.md`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `pyod-master/examples/auto_model_selection_example/with_note_selection_results/output.json`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `pyod-master/examples/auto_model_selection_example/without_note_selection_results/output.json`: (Fő feldolgozó / LLM modul)
  - 📄 `pyod-master/examples/cblof_example.py`: *Example of using Cluster-based Local Outlier Factor (CBLOF) for outlier detection*
  - 📄 `pyod-master/examples/cd_example.py`: *Example of using Cook's distance (CD) for outlier detection*
  - 📄 `pyod-master/examples/cof_example.py`: *Example of using Connectivity-Based Outlier Factor (COF)  for outlier detection*

## 📦 REPO: python-mss-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # xcbproto Directory This directory contains the tooling and protocol definitions used to generate Python bindings for XCB (X C Binding). ## Overview - **`gen_xcb_to_py.py`**: Code generator that produces Python/ctypes bindings from XCB protocol XML files. - **`*.xml`**: Protocol definition files vendored from the upstream [xcbproto](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/xorg/proto/xcbproto) repository. These describe the X11 core protocol and extensions (RandR, Render, XFixes, etc.). ## Workflow

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**

  - 📄 `rl-baselines3-zoo-master/rl_zoo3/push_to_hub.py`: *Saves a model card for the repository.      :param repo_dir: repository directory     :param generated_model_card: model card generated by _generate_model_card()     :param metadata: metadata*

  - 📄 `rl-baselines3-zoo-master/rl_zoo3/wrappers.py`: *Reset on success and offsets the reward.     Useful for GoalEnv.*
  - 📄 `rl-baselines3-zoo-master/scripts/create_cluster_jobs.py`: *Send multiple jobs to the cluster.*
  - 📄 `rl-baselines3-zoo-master/scripts/run_jobs.py`: *Run multiple experiments on a single machine.*
  - 📄 `rl-baselines3-zoo-master/setup.py`: *# RL Baselines3 Zoo: A Training Framework for Stable Baselines3 Reinforcement Learning Agents  See https://github.com/DLR-RM/rl-baselines3-zoo*

## 📦 REPO: swift-composable-architecture-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # The Composable Architecture The Composable Architecture (TCA, for short) is a library for building applications in a consistent and understandable way, with composition, testing, and ergonomics in mind. It can be used in SwiftUI, UIKit, and more, and on any Apple platform (iOS, macOS, iPadOS, visionOS, tvOS, and watchOS).

## 📦 REPO: tailscale-main
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Lightweight macOS VM's for tstest and natlab This utility is designed to provide custom virtual machine tooling support for macOS.  The intent is to quickly create and spin up small, preconfigured virtual machines, for executing integration and unit tests. The primary driver is to provide support for VZVirtioNetworkDeviceConfiguration which is not supported by other popular macOS VM hosts.  This also gives us the freedom to fully customize and script all virtual machine setup and interaction. ...

## 📦 REPO: undetected-chromedriver-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** # undetected_chromedriver # https://github.com/ultrafunkamsterdam/undetected-chromedriver Optimized Selenium Chromedriver patch which does not trigger anti-bot services like Distill Network / Imperva / DataDome / Botprotect.io Automatically downloads the driver binary and patches it. * Tested until current chrome beta versions * Works also on Brave Browser and many other Chromium based browsers, but you need to know what you're doing and needs some tweaking.

**Kritikus Fájlok és Szerepük:**
  - 📄 `undetected-chromedriver-master/example/example.py`: *let container = document.querySelector('#rso');         let el = document.createElement('div');         el.style = 'width:500px;display:block;background:red;color:white;z-index:999;transition:all 2s e...*
  - 📄 `undetected-chromedriver-master/setup.py`: *888                                                  888         d8b          888                                                  888         Y8P          888                                         ...*
  - 📄 `undetected-chromedriver-master/undetected_chromedriver/__init__.py`: *888                                                  888         d8b          888                                                  888         Y8P          888                                         ...*
  - 📄 `undetected-chromedriver-master/undetected_chromedriver/devtool.py`: *This is a dict-like object structure, which you should subclass     Only properties defined in the class context are used on initialization.      See example*
  - 📄 `undetected-chromedriver-master/undetected_chromedriver/dprocess.py`: *Starts a fully independent subprocess (with no parent)     :param executable: executable     :param args: arguments to the executable, eg: ['--param1_key=param1_val', '-vvv' ...]     :return: pid of t...*
  - 📄 `undetected-chromedriver-master/undetected_chromedriver/options.py`: *Sets the browser profile folder to use, or creates a new profile         at given <path>.          Parameters         ----------         path: str             the path to a chrome profile folder      ...*
  - 📄 `undetected-chromedriver-master/undetected_chromedriver/patcher.py`: *Args:             executable_path: None = automatic                              a full file path to the chromedriver executable             force: False                     terminate processes which ...*
  - 📄 `undetected-chromedriver-master/undetected_chromedriver/reactor.py`: *Parameters         ----------         event_name: str             example "Network.responseReceived"          callback: callable             callable which accepts 1 parameter: the message object dict...*
  - 📄 `undetected-chromedriver-master/undetected_chromedriver/webelement.py`: *returns direct child elements of current element         :param tag: str,  if supplied, returns <tag> nodes only*

## 📦 REPO: wireshark-master
**Funkció / Leírás:** # Asterix parser generator *Asterix* is a set of standards, where each standard is defined as so called *asterix category*. In addition, each *asterix category* is potentially released in number of editions. Normally, newer editions of the same category are backward compatible, however in general, there is no such guarantie. Structured version of asterix specifications is maintained in a separate project: <https://zoranbosnjak.github.io/asterix-specs/specs.html>

**Funkció / Leírás:** <a href="https://www.ultralytics.com/"><img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ultralytics/assets/main/logo/Ultralytics_Logotype_Original.svg" width="320" alt="Ultralytics logo"></a> <img src="https://cdn.comet.ml/img/notebook_logo.png"> # Using Ultralytics YOLO With Comet Welcome to the guide for integrating [Ultralytics YOLO](https://github.com/ultralytics/yolov5) with [Comet](https://www.comet.com/site/)! Comet offers robust experiment tracking, model management, and visualization tools t...
